mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500 are negative or flat (less than 0.1% average) during periods of sustained RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market, which typically leads to increased wage growth and consumer confidence, providing a tailwind for equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation remain low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy environment that reduces the cost of capital and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 238.111 as of 2015-11-01
failure_condition: If the correlation between the product of (1/FEDFUNDS) and (1/CPIAUCSL) and S&P 500 20-day returns is not positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts during an economic recovery period signals renewed investment and consumer confidence in the real estate sector, which typically precedes broader economic strength and positive short-term equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1060.0 (thousands of units) as of 2015-10-01
failure_condition: If the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative following a HOUST increase of more than 5% in a recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below a critical threshold indicates weakening manufacturing activity and potential recessionary pressures, leading to a decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1% over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 1% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142900.0 (thousands of persons) as of 2015-11-01
failure_condition: If the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and the Federal Reserve begins to raise the federal funds rate, it signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which increases borrowing costs and negatively impacts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 3 months AND UNRATE is below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2015-11-01
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in real GDP growth below a healthy expansion rate signals a weakening economic environment, leading to reduced corporate profit forecasts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16417.8 (Billions of Chained 2017 Dollars) as of 2015-07-01
failure_condition: If the average S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive following a period where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%.
